fields.name: Diabetes
fields.description: Diabetes is a chronic metabolic disease where blood glucose levels are too high because the body doesn't make enough insulin or doesn't use insulin effectively. Symptoms can include increased thirst, frequent urination, and unexplained weight loss, while long-term complications can affect the heart, eyes, kidneys, and nerves. Type 1 diabetes involves the body producing little to no insulin, while Type 2 diabetes involves insulin resistance or insufficient insulin production.

fields.question: What should I expect during this treatment?
fields.answer: This treatment typically involves several stages including consultation, preparation, the procedure itself, and recovery. Your medical team will guide you through each step.

fields.question: How long is the recovery period?
fields.answer: Recovery time varies by individual, but most patients can expect to return to normal activities within 2-3 weeks.

fields.question: What are the risks associated with this treatment?
fields.answer: Like any medical procedure, this treatment carries some risks. Your doctor will discuss all potential risks and benefits with you during your consultation.

fields.question: How long does the CABG surgery take?
fields.answer: The surgery typically takes 3-6 hours depending on the complexity and number of bypasses required.

fields.question: What is the success rate for CABG surgery?
fields.answer: CABG has a high success rate of over 95% with most patients experiencing significant improvement in symptoms.

fields.question: How long do knee replacements last?
fields.answer: Modern knee replacements typically last 15-20 years, with many patients experiencing longer durability.

fields.question: When can I resume normal activities after knee replacement?
fields.answer: Most patients can walk with assistance within 24 hours after surgery and resume normal daily activities within 3-6 weeks.
